name: Zaccharie Risacher
notes: Size-and-shooting wing whose upside rests on creation growth.

name: Alex Sarr
notes: Movement and rim defense made sense, but offensive certainty was still early-stage.

name: Reed Sheppard
notes: Elite efficiency guard with defensive playmaking and feel.

name: Stephon Castle
notes: Size, defense, and connective offensive feel created a clear role foundation.

name: Ron Holland
notes: Motor and downhill pressure are real, but the jumper shapes the entire ceiling.

name: Tidjane Salaun
notes: Age, frame, and mobility are attractive; on-ball polish is still early.

name: Donovan Clingan
notes: Rim protection and interior size create a strong role floor.

name: Rob Dillingham
notes: Creation craft and pull-up shotmaking are real; physical/defensive tolerance is the question.

name: Cody Williams
notes: Length and touch are intriguing, but strength and creation volume still need time.

name: Matas Buzelis
notes: Size-and-skill case is appealing, but the frame and shot readiness still matter a lot.

name: Nikola Topic
notes: Big lead guard indicators are there, but health and defensive pressure shape the final range.

name: Devin Carter
notes: Older guard model, but event creation and competitive edge were strong role indicators.

name: Dalton Knecht
notes: Older prospect, but the shooting and scoring translation case was clean.

name: Ja'Kobe Walter
notes: The scoring tools are real, but efficiency and broader feel still determine the final tier.

name: Brandon Miller
notes: Movement shooter plus size-driven self-creation upside.

name: Amen Thompson
notes: Explosive transition and paint-touch creator with jumper risk.

name: Ausar Thompson
notes: Wing-size athlete with defensive value and connective offense.

name: Anthony Black
notes: Size, defense, and connective feel were attractive, but scoring gravity remained uncertain.

name: Jarace Walker
notes: Strength and defensive playmaking translated, but shot and advantage creation remained swing variables.

name: Taylor Hendricks
notes: High-value modern frontcourt indicators with fewer on-ball demands.

name: Cason Wallace
notes: Decision speed, shot quality, and point-of-attack defense created a dependable guard value profile.

name: Dereck Lively II
notes: College production was muted, but the role-specific defensive tools were easy to trust.

name: Keyonte George
notes: Shot difficulty and self-creation were real, but efficiency and defensive floor stayed unstable.

name: Paolo Banchero
notes: Creation-forward archetype built on size, touch, and mismatch offense.

name: Chet Holmgren
notes: Elite rim deterrence with enough skill to stay additive on offense.

name: Jabari Smith Jr.
notes: Shotmaking size was premium, but self-creation limits capped the top-end offensive pathway.

name: Keegan Murray
notes: Older but clean production and shooting/finishing mix translated into a stable high-value role.

name: Jaden Ivey
notes: Explosive advantage creation showed up quickly, while decision consistency remained uneven.

name: Bennedict Mathurin
notes: Vertical pressure, shooting, and transition pop gave him a cleaner scoring-wing floor.

name: Shaedon Sharpe
notes: Upside case was rooted in athletic scoring talent more than a stable evidence base.

name: Jalen Williams
notes: Late-lottery value model for wings with feel, size, and efficiency.

name: Walker Kessler
notes: Outcome reminder for elite shot-block indicators translating quickly.

name: Cade Cunningham
notes: Large on-ball initiator with real shotmaking and pace control.

name: Jalen Green
notes: Shot creation and athletic burst were obvious, but the all-around floor stayed fragile.

name: Evan Mobley
notes: Mobile frontcourt defender whose value scales with coverage range.

name: Scottie Barnes
notes: Playmaking size and defensive versatility created a broad role floor with star paths.

name: Jalen Suggs
notes: Competitive two-way guard with some creation juice, but the offensive ceiling stayed below top-tier outcomes.

name: Josh Giddey
notes: Passing size and vision were real, but shooting and defensive limitations narrowed the lineup ceiling.

name: Franz Wagner
notes: Feel, size, and scalable wing skill translated into much more than a connector outcome.

name: Anthony Edwards
notes: Power athlete scorer whose decision growth unlocked star-level creation.

name: LaMelo Ball
notes: Size, passing imagination, and pace control carried the bet despite efficiency variance.

name: Patrick Williams
notes: Tools and switch value were clear, but the offensive role path stayed thinner than hoped.

name: Isaac Okoro
notes: Strength and on-ball defense translated, but offensive scalability remained the pressure point.

name: Tyrese Haliburton
notes: Elite feel and efficiency profile drastically outperformed draft slot expectations.
